measure.note: broadened from the original `\?\?\s*0` (adversarial-pass finding): that pattern only caught a LITERAL `?? 0` and was silently evadable by `?? -1` or `?? 0.0` (or any other numeric fallback) — same silent-default class the red flags ban ('a cue missing from the budget map gets a hidden default'). `-?\d` matches any signed numeric literal immediately after `??`, anywhere in the file.

prompt.md line 22 bans Record<string, number> for VISUAL_MOTION_SECONDS — an index-signature type erases the literal cue-id keys, so keyof typeof degrades to bare `string` and the whole cue-id-union compile-time safety net silently stops working. REAL historical drift: _prior-runs/kptest-fenyuhe-six/pre-suffix-20260612/kptestFenyuheSixLessonTimeline.ts used exactly this pattern pre-c487c5e. BROADENED (adversarial-pass finding): the original `Record<string,\s*number>` only matched that ONE exact spelling — evadable by whitespace variants (`Record< string , number >`) or by the equivalent TS index-signature shape `{[k: string]: number}`, which erases the keys identically but never spells `Record<`. The pattern now covers both forms.
prompt.md line 16/25: the OTHER-direction cross-check (a stale/renamed budget id throws) — reconcileClipTimeline only throws when a CLIP has no budget, not the reverse.
the ONE symbol scene/wrapper code resolves every cue id through (prompt.md line 16) — missing it is a downstream compile break at w4a/w4b, caught here first.

ADVERSARIAL-PASS FIX: every check ABOVE is keyed on a `path` containing the state-channel token for camelLessonId, which `resolveTokens` (workflow/resolver.ts) THROWS `MissingChannelError` for whenever a run's `.pi/state.json` lacks that key — `runSubstrateMeasure` catches it per-op (never crashes the whole report) but the effect is that ALL 13 checks above land in `ops.rejected` and NONE fires, i.e. the entire hard floor for this node goes dark on exactly the runs most likely to need it (an interrupted/partial run, or one that predates full state-promote wiring). This is REAL, not hypothetical, in THIS repo today: `.piflow/lesson-build/runs/{count-to-two-001,dryrun-kp4,e10-dryrun,e10e11-accept-1,kp3-tens-and-ones-place-r1,kp3-tens-and-ones-place-r2,kp3-tens-and-ones-place-r3,prognode-validate,prognode-validate2,prognode-validate3,salted-zelnik}/.pi/state.json` are ALL literally `{}` (verified). This op takes ONLY `{{RUN}}`/`{{WORKSPACE}}` — tokens that ALWAYS resolve (`resolveTokens` never throws for `RUN`/`WORKSPACE`) — so it survives regardless of `state.json`. `scripts/measure.mjs` derives the concrete `<Camel>LessonTimeline.ts` path itself from `<RUN>/.pi/run.json`'s OWN already-resolved `nodes['w3-5-reconcile'].artifacts[].path` (the physical path the SDK computed successfully at real node-launch time, mirroring the SAME `discoverLessonId`-off-run.json move `w2b-audio-captions/scripts/measure.mjs` already uses for the identical class of gap) — falling back to `state.json`'s `camelLessonId` when run.json's own artifact entry is itself empty (a `reused` node with no recorded artifacts). It then re-runs the SAME 13 mirrored invariants above (the identical broadened patterns, including the non-empty check) against that robustly-resolved path, so the floor still FIRES even when every token-keyed check above is rejected. FAIL-CLOSED: the script exits 1 (surfaces as `ops.runs[].failed:true`) on ANY re-checked invariant failing OR on a resolved-but-missing file; it writes an explicit `resolved:false` (never a bare pass) when NEITHER run.json nor state.json yields a path — triage sees a recorded skip, never a silent green. Never throws (best-effort throughout, per Part D of building-measures.md).
